caption:
Let's talk about the math your brokerage doesn't want you to see.

If you closed 20 deals last year at a $10K average commission, you earned $200K gross.

At a traditional 70/30 split, you kept $140K.
At a 80/20 split with franchise fees, maybe $148K.

At LPT Realty on the Business Builder plan? $500 per transaction. That's $10K in fees total.

You keep $190K.

That's a $42K-$50K difference. Same effort, same clients, same closings.

Run your own numbers and see what you'd actually keep.

#RealEstate #100PercentCommission #BrokerageSwitch #RealtorLife #TPLCollective #LPTRealty

notes: Strong opener for the month. Lead with math, not hype.

$5K annual cap. Once you hit it, you're done paying.

caption:
How does LPT Realty's fee structure actually work? Here's the simple version.

Business Builder Plan:
- 100% commission on every deal
- $500 flat transaction fee
- $5,000 annual cap on those fees
- Once you cap, $0 to the brokerage for the rest of the year

No royalty fees. No franchise fees. No desk fees eating into your check.

TPL Collective is how we support agents making the switch - AI tools, lead gen systems, and a community that actually helps you produce.

Questions? That's literally what we're here for.

#LPTRealty #TPLCollective #RealEstate #BrokerageSwitch #RealEstateAgent #100PercentCommission
image_concept: Clean infographic showing the fee structure as a simple flow chart. $500 per deal arrow leading to $5K cap, then $0 after cap.
notes: Educational post - straightforward, no hard sell.

caption:
Your brokerage split isn't the only thing costing you money.

Add up what you're really paying:
- Commission split (20-30% of every check)
- Desk fees ($200-$800/month)
- Technology fees ($50-$150/month)
- Franchise fees (3-8% off the top)
- E&O insurance markup
- Transaction coordinator fees

Most agents at big-name brokerages are giving away $25K-$50K per year in fees they don't even think about.

There's a reason agents keep switching to flat-fee models. The math is hard to argue with.

#RealEstate #RealtorLife #BrokerageSwitch #RealEstateAgent #TPLCollective
image_concept: Stacked bar chart showing hidden fees at traditional brokerages vs. the single flat fee at LPT. Red bars stacking up on the left, one small green bar on the right.
notes: Pain point post. Let agents feel the weight of what they're paying.

"I kept an extra $27K last year. Same number of deals. Same market. I just stopped giving away a third of my income."

That's not a typo. $27K more in the bank from doing the exact same work.

#TPLCollective #LPTRealty #RealEstate #BrokerageSwitch #RealtorLife #100PercentCommission

caption:
If you close one deal per month at a $350K sale price, here's what that looks like.

At 3% commission = $10,500 per deal.

70/30 split: You keep $7,350. Brokerage keeps $3,150.
80/20 split: You keep $8,400. Brokerage keeps $2,100.
$500 flat fee: You keep $10,000. Brokerage gets $500.

Over 12 deals that's:
- 70/30 = $88,200 kept
- 80/20 = $100,800 kept
- Flat fee = $120,000 kept

Same production. $20K-$32K difference.

#RealEstate #RealtorLife #100PercentCommission #BrokerageSwitch #TPLCollective #LPTRealty
image_concept: Three columns showing the annual math for each split scenario. Green highlight on the flat fee column. Clean spreadsheet-style layout.
notes: Monday post - start the week with hard numbers.

caption:
What is revenue share and why does it matter?

At LPT Realty, when you refer agents to the brokerage, you earn a percentage of the company's revenue from their transactions. Not from their commission - from the company's portion.

This creates a passive income stream that grows as your network grows. Some agents are earning $5K-$10K+ per month just from rev share.

TPL Collective helps you build that network strategically with real tools and systems, not just "invite your friends."

#LPTRealty #TPLCollective #RealEstate #RevenueShare #PassiveIncome #RealEstateAgent
image_concept: Simple diagram showing how revenue share flows: Agent refers new agent, company revenue trickles back to original agent. Tree-like visual.
notes: Educational but exciting. Revenue share is a major draw.

caption:
Franchise fees are the silent killer of agent income.

Keller Williams: 6% franchise fee on every deal.
Coldwell Banker: 6-8% franchise fee.
Century 21: 6% franchise fee.
RE/MAX: up to 5% plus monthly dues.

That's ON TOP of your commission split.

So when your brokerage says "80/20 split" - it's really more like 72/28 after franchise fees.

LPT Realty has zero franchise fees. $500 per deal. Period.

#RealEstate #BrokerageSwitch #RealtorLife #FranchiseFees #TPLCollective #100PercentCommission
image_concept: Comparison table showing franchise fees by brokerage. Red percentages for big brands, green $0 for LPT. Clean, factual layout.
notes: Franchise fee awareness post. This is a pain point most agents don't fully understand.

caption:
"I was paying $1,400/month just in desk fees and tech fees at my old brokerage. Before I even sold a house."

That's $16,800 a year gone before your first closing.

At LPT Realty you pay when you close. Not before. Not whether you sell or not. When you close.

The model just makes more sense.

#RealEstate #TPLCollective #LPTRealty #BrokerageSwitch #RealtorLife

caption:
Monday reminder: You don't owe your brokerage loyalty. They don't owe you anything either.

This is a business. Run it like one.

If a vendor charged you 25% of your revenue for services you could get for a flat $500 - you'd fire them immediately.

Your brokerage split is no different. It's a business expense. And it might be your biggest one.

Time to audit your P&L.

#RealEstate #RealtorLife #BrokerageSwitch #RealEstateAgent #TPLCollective
image_concept: Bold text on dark background: 'Your split is a business expense. Treat it like one.' Professional, no-nonsense design.
notes: Mindset shift post. Monday energy.

caption:
Tax day reality check.

After splits, franchise fees, desk fees, tech fees, marketing costs, gas, and self-employment tax - most agents keep about 40-45 cents of every dollar they earn.

You can't control taxes. You can't control gas prices. But you CAN control how much your brokerage takes.

Switching from a 70/30 split to a $500 flat fee could put $20K-$40K back in your pocket. That's real money.

#RealEstate #TaxDay #RealtorLife #BrokerageSwitch #100PercentCommission #TPLCollective
image_concept: Pie chart showing where an agent's gross commission dollar actually goes. Brokerage slice highlighted as the one you can reduce.
notes: Tax day tie-in. Timely and relevant.

caption:
People ask: "What's the difference between TPL Collective and LPT Realty?"

LPT Realty is the brokerage. Cloud-based, 100% commission model, $500 per deal, growing fast nationwide.

TPL Collective is the team and community layer we built on top of LPT. We provide:
- AI-powered tools for lead gen and follow-up
- Recruiting systems and support
- A real community (not just a Slack channel)
- Hands-on mentorship

Think of it this way: LPT gives you the best fee structure in real estate. TPL gives you the support system to actually crush it.

caption:
The "80/20 split" at your brokerage isn't really 80/20.

Here's what it actually looks like after all the fees:

$10,000 commission
- 20% split = -$2,000
- 6% franchise fee = -$600
- E&O fee = -$50
- Tech fee = -$75
- Transaction fee = -$300

You keep: $6,975 (69.75%)

At LPT: $10,000 - $500 = $9,500 (95%)

That's a $2,525 difference on a single deal. Multiply that by your annual volume.

#RealEstate #BrokerageSwitch #RealtorLife #100PercentCommission #TPLCollective #RealEstateAgent
image_concept: Receipt-style graphic showing line-item deductions from a $10K commission at a traditional brokerage vs. LPT. Running total at bottom.
notes: The 'receipt' concept hits hard because agents feel every deduction.

caption:

RE/MAX model: 95/5 split sounds great - until you add the $2,000-$3,000/month in desk fees, advertising fees, and monthly dues.

That's $24K-$36K per year in overhead before your split even kicks in.

LPT model: $500 per deal, $5K annual cap, zero monthly fees.

Agent closing 15 deals/year:
- RE/MAX: $30K+ in total fees
- LPT: $5,000 (capped)

The "95/5 split" is a mirage when the fees are this high.

#RealEstate #BrokerageSwitch #REMAX #LPTRealty #TPLCollective #100PercentCommission

caption:
FAQ: "Is switching brokerages hard?"

Honest answer: It takes about a week of paperwork. That's it.

Your clients don't care what brokerage logo is on your card. They hired YOU. Your relationships, your CRM data, your sphere - all of that goes with you.

The hardest part isn't the switch. The hardest part is admitting your current brokerage is costing you more than it's giving you.

TPL Collective walks you through every step of the transition.

#RealEstate #BrokerageSwitch #TPLCollective #RealtorLife #RealEstateAgent
